objective: Architect sign-off on the OpenAI-compatible chat client + health probe in core::explore::client, verifying architectural coherence and deployment posture.
key_changes: No new dependency: core/Cargo.toml zero diff; built on existing ureq 2 (rustls) from architecture/stack.md, serde_json::Value for tool schemas.
Correct seam: ChatClient trait is the T03/T04 dependency point, never the concrete OpenAiCompatClient — swappable and testable.
Typed errors serve runtime: ClientError::Connection (ureq Transport) distinguished from Protocol/Http to feed the D3 shutdown signal in the agent loop.
Dialect normalization localized: Ollama string-args vs llama.cpp object-args absorbed in-module, uniform ToolCall to callers.
Blocking defect resolved: char-boundary-safe truncate() walk-back eliminates multi-byte UTF-8 panic, with regression test.
Scope clean: only client.rs (new) + mod.rs (wiring/re-exports) in the code surface; no other module touched.
No persisted schema/config migration: all new types are in-memory transport types; task only reads ExploreConfig (T01).
No operational impact, no version bump — additive non-material change.
Follow-up: model_available() substring matching can false-positive (llama3 vs llama31) — tolerant-by-design, revisit only if needed.
Follow-up: non-2xx /models maps to HealthError::Unreachable; add finer variant if T04 needs server-up-but-erroring distinction.
Follow-up: non-streaming only by design; streaming is a separate future task if ever required.
